perturbation.original_text: 6.7. The Counterparty must inform Eskom i n writing of any existing or potential conflict of interest with the interests of Eskom pursuant to this Project . Where any potential or actual conflict of interests exists, the prospective f irm shall immediately notify Eskom thereof in writing. Eskom at its sole discretion may elect whether the Counterparty can continue with the Project fully or partially and to the extent the Counterparty cannot continue with the Project it will renounce any benefits it may have obtained had it completed the Project and will be paid only for work completed. The omission of such information or the making of any false statements or representations shall entitle Eskom to terminate all contracts with the Counterparty regarding the Project and to invoke all remedies available to it in law .
perturbation.changed_text:
23. PUBLICITY
Neither Party will make or issue any formal or informal announcement or statement to the press or any Third Party in connection with this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party.

6.7. The Counterparty must inform Eskom i n writing of any existing or potential conflict of interest with the interests of Eskom pursuant to this Project . Where any potential or actual conflict of interests exists, the prospective f irm shall immediately notify Eskom thereof in writing. Eskom at its sole discretion may elect whether the Counterparty can continue with the Project fully or partially and to the extent the Counterparty cannot continue with the Project it will renounce any benefits it may have obtained had it completed the Project and will be paid only for work completed. The omission of such information or the making of any false statements or representations shall entitle Eskom to terminate all contracts with the Counterparty regarding the Project and to invoke all remedies available to it in law .
perturbation.explanation: Moving the conflict of interest disclosure to the 'Publicity' section buries a critical contractual obligation within an unrelated clause, making it less likely to be noticed and enforced. This creates a structural flaw that could allow conflicts of interest to go unreported.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Prevention and Combating of Corrupt Activities Act, 2004 (Act No. 12 of 2004)
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 34(1) of the Prevention and Combating of Corrupt Activities Act places a duty on certain persons to report corrupt transactions. By burying the conflict of interest disclosure in an unrelated section, the agreement obscures this duty and potentially hinders the reporting of corrupt activities. The location should be very visible and not hidden in a section that is unrelated to ethics or conflict of interest.

perturbation.justification: The perturbation involves a structural flaw where a conflict of interest disclosure requirement is moved to the 'Publicity' section, making it less visible and potentially hindering its enforcement. The LLM's Law Explanation highlights that Section 34(1) of the Prevention and Combating of Corrupt Activities Act places a duty on certain persons to report corrupt transactions. By burying the conflict of interest disclosure, the agreement obscures this duty and potentially hinders the reporting of corrupt activities. While the scraped snippets are not directly relevant to this act, the logic stands that by burying the disclosure in an unrelated section it weakens the enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 20.  DOMICILIA AND NOTICE S 20.1. The Parties hereby choose domicilium citandi et executandi ("domicilium") for all purposes under the Agreement as follows – Eskom –  Physical Address - Megawatt Park, Maxwell Drive, Sunninghill;  Postal Address - PO Box 1091, Johannesburg, 2000, South Africa  Telephone No - +27 11 ____ _____________  Fax No - +27 11 _______________________  Contact Person - _________________ (_________ ) [Counterparty]  Physical Address ________________________________________ __________________________________________________________  Postal Address -_______ ___________________________________ __________________________________________________________  Telephone No - __________________________________________  Fax No - _______________________________________________  Contact Person and Designation - _____________ ______________ __________________________________________________________ 20.2. Any notice given by one party to the other is deemed to have been received by the addressee: 20.2.1. on the date on which the same was delivered to the addressee's address if delivered by ha nd; or 20.2.2. on the 7th (seventh) calendar day after the date of posting if sent by pre - paid registered post to the addressee's address; or 20.2.3. on dispatch, if sent to the addressee's then telefax number. 20.3. A party may change that party's addresses for this purpose, by notice in writing to the other party, such a change of address being effective 7 (seven) days after the deemed receipt by the addressee of such written notice. A similar notice will also be required in respect of new or changed telephone and telefax nu mbers.
perturbation.changed_text:
3. RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE PARTIES
3.1. Neither this Agreement nor the exchange of information contemplated hereby shall commit either Party to continue d iscussions or to negotiate, or to be legally bound to any potential business relationship. The Parties shall only be bound to a business relationship by way of further definitive written agreements signed by the Parties.
3.2. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as –
3.2.1. prohibiting either Party from entering into a business relationship with any Third Party ;
3.2.2. creating a joint venture, partnership or employment relationship between the Parties and n either Party shall have the right, power or implied authority to create any obligation or duty (express, implied or otherwise) on behalf of the other Party. For the avoidance of doubt, nothing in this Agreement shall oblige either of the Parties to enter into any agreements or transactions whatsoever.
20.  DOMICILIA AND NOTICE S 20.1. The Parties hereby choose domicilium citandi et executandi ("domicilium") for all purposes under the Agreement as follows – Eskom –  Physical Address - Megawatt Park, Maxwell Drive, Sunninghill;  Postal Address - PO Box 1091, Johannesburg, 2000, South Africa
20.2. Any notice given by one party to the other is deemed to have been received by the addressee: 20.2.1. on the date on which the same was delivered to the addressee's address if delivered by ha nd; or 20.2.2. on the 7th (seventh) calendar day after the date of posting if sent by pre - paid registered post to the addressee's address; or 20.2.3. on dispatch, if sent to the addressee's then telefax number. 20.3. A party may change that party's addresses for this purpose, by notice in writing to the other party, such a change of address being effective 7 (seven) days after the deemed receipt by the addressee of such written notice. A similar notice will also be required in respect of new or changed telephone and telefax nu mbers.
perturbation.explanation: Relocating the 'Domicilia and Notices' clause to the 'Relationship Between the Parties' section obscures crucial information about how legal notices should be served. By obfuscating this section, it makes it much harder for either party to properly inform the other of essential information regarding breach of contract, and legal proceedings, greatly affecting enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_law: High Court Rule 4(1)(a) of South Africa
perturbation.law_explanation: The High Court Rule 4(1)(a) requires proper service of legal documents at the chosen domicilium citandi et executandi. By placing the clause specifying the domicilium in an unexpected and unrelated section (Relationship between the Parties), one party may claim that notice was not properly served because the clause was not reasonably accessible, potentially invalidating legal proceedings. This is because proper notice is a requirement of natural justice under South African Law.

perturbation.justification: The changed text obscures the 'Domicilia and Notices' clause by relocating it to an unrelated section, 'Relationship Between the Parties.' The Law Explanation indicates this may lead to a claim that notice was not properly served, potentially invalidating legal proceedings under South African law. While the scraped snippets do not directly confirm this, the provided information overall suggests a structural flaw that undermines the enforceability of the notice provision, creating a legal contradiction as the notice clause may not be reasonably accessible.
